summary: Completed testing of Smart Dropdowns feature in Hero section. All 14 backend API tests passed (100%) and all frontend UI tests passed. The city dropdown shows only cities with businesses (with counts), has a search bar for filtering, and the service dropdown dynamically filters based on selected city.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/cities?country_code=MX&with_businesses=true returns only cities with businesses - PASSED
Backend: Cities with businesses are sorted by business_count descending - PASSED
Backend: CDMX has 3 businesses in the response - PASSED
Backend: Without with_businesses param returns all seeded cities - PASSED
Backend: City search filter works with with_businesses - PASSED
Backend: GET /api/categories returns all 8 categories - PASSED
Backend: GET /api/categories?city=CDMX returns only categories with businesses in CDMX - PASSED
Backend: CDMX has 'Belleza y Estética' category - PASSED
Backend: Non-existent city returns empty categories - PASSED
Backend: Categories include business_count field - PASSED
Backend: Search with city param filters businesses - PASSED
Backend: Search with query param works - PASSED
Backend: Full flow city to categories integration - PASSED
Backend: All cities option returns all categories - PASSED
Frontend: City dropdown opens with search bar - PASSED
Frontend: City dropdown shows only cities with businesses (CDMX 3, test 1, Test City 1) - PASSED
Frontend: City search filters cities in real-time - PASSED
Frontend: Selecting CDMX filters service dropdown to show only 'Belleza y Estética' - PASSED
Frontend: 'Todas las ciudades' clears city filter - PASSED
Frontend: Without city filter, service dropdown shows all 8 categories - PASSED
Frontend: Search button navigates to /search with q and city params - PASSED
critical_code_review_comments: HomePage.jsx correctly uses heroCities state (loaded from /api/cities?with_businesses=true) for the city dropdown
City dropdown has search bar with data-testid='city-search-input' for filtering
Cities show business_count in the dropdown (e.g., 'CDMX 3 negocios')
Service dropdown uses filteredCategories when city is selected, falls back to categories (all) when no city
Backend /api/cities endpoint correctly supports with_businesses=true parameter
Backend /api/categories endpoint correctly filters by city and country_code parameters
Both dropdowns use refs for outside-click detection to close

mocked_api_note.Cloudinary: Falls back to Emergent Object Storage

test_credentials: No credentials needed - tested public homepage and APIs
seed_data_creation: None - used existing seeded data (CDMX with 3 businesses, test with 1, Test City with 1)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Smart Dropdowns feature is fully implemented and tested. Key points: (1) City dropdown in hero uses heroCities state loaded from /api/cities?with_businesses=true, (2) City dropdown has search bar with data-testid='city-search-input', (3) Cities show business counts (e.g., 'CDMX 3 negocios'), (4) Service dropdown uses filteredCategories when city selected (loaded from /api/categories?city=X&country_code=MX), (5) Without city selection, service dropdown shows all categories, (6) Search navigates to /search?q=X&city=Y, (7) Backend endpoints: GET /api/cities?country_code=MX&with_businesses=true and GET /api/categories?city=CDMX&country_code=MX
